title: Anogra pallida

This family includes over 1,600 species, many of which are found in Africa and the Mediterranean region. Anogra pallida is further classified within the subfamily Asphodeloideae, which contains many species of asphodels and other related plants. intro: Anogra pallida, also known as Anogeissus latifolia, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Combretaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and rheumatism, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Anogra pallida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Anogra pallida is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies, such as tonics and syrups.
